source_pr.title: config: remove the pessimistic.ttl configure
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

The default value of pessimistic.ttl in the TiDB old version is 40, and the permitted value in the TiDB new version is 3~30

If we do not handle it, TiDB rolling update would fail.

### What is changed and how it works?

Remove pessimistic.ttl configure to make the TiDB rolling update process smooth.

Now PessimisticLockTTL is a 20s constant, without config to change it.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)

I manually test using new TiDB with the old configure file, there is a WARNING but everything goes well.

```
➜  bin git:(master) ✗ ./tidb-server -config ../config/config.toml.example
[2019/11/04 14:03:14.790 +08:00] [WARN] [main.go:170] ["config file ../config/config.toml.example contained unknown configuration options: pessimistic-txn.ttl"]
```


Side effects

 - Breaking backward compatibility

Related changes

 - Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

Release note

 - Write release note for bug-fix or new feature.

symptoms: Go race detector reports a DATA RACE caused by global `PessimisticLockTTL` being written during test setup.
failure_signature: DATA RACE detected under `-race`, involving concurrent access to the global `PessimisticLockTTL` (written in suite/test setup in `store/tikv/2pc_test.go`).
root_cause_explanation: The flakiness is due to a real data race on shared global state: the tests mutate the global `PessimisticLockTTL` in setup, which can race with other concurrent test execution under `-race`. The PR stabilizes it by moving the global assignment into `SetUpSuite` (instead of per-test setup) and ensuring suite-level initialization is done once, avoiding concurrent global writes.
fix_pattern: Avoid data races on global variables in tests: move global initialization to suite-level setup (run once) instead of per-test setup so concurrent tests don't write shared state.

evidence: files: config/config.go, config/config.toml.example, config/config_test.go, store/tikv/2pc.go, store/tikv/2pc_test.go, tidb-server/main.go
